question_text: Let X be a complete metric space and {On} be a countable collection of dense open subsets of X . Prove that the union ∪ On is not empty.

question_text: Let A and B be metric spaces. Prove that f: A ➜➜➜➜ B is continuous if and only if f −−−−1(V) is an open set in A whenever V is open in B.
